key_items: Bitcoin breaks below $62,000 as $2 trillion erased from crypto market cap with sellers maintaining control
Capital rotation into AI infrastructure represents $400 billion shift away from Bitcoin, pressuring BTC price
Standard Chartered identifies three triggers for new Bitcoin lows while miners face $11B+ paper losses alongside major holders
reasoning: Bitcoin faces a sharp near-term headwind from sustained capital rotation into AI equities, with the $60K support level under acute pressure and major holders like MicroStrategy recording massive unrealized losses that could trigger forced liquidation cascades. However, bullish structural catalysts including Fannie Mae-backed Bitcoin mortgages, miners pivoting to AI infrastructure monetization, and technical support near mining cost basis ($60K) suggest this bearish pressure may be temporary, preventing a -7 crash-level assessment.
risks: A break below $60,000 support could trigger cascading liquidations and accelerate capital flight to AI equities, particularly if broader equity markets weaken and reduce risk appetite across all asset classes.
